prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "直腿跳" (straight-leg pogo hops) exercise, side view, facing right, freeze-frame photographic style. Person posture: Photographic freeze-frame of a pogo-style stiff-leg jump, mid-air. The athlete is suspended ABOVE THE GROUND with about 20 cm of CLEAR EMPTY FLOOR SPACE visible directly below both feet (you can see the floor and a small shadow far below). BOTH KNEES ARE LOCKED COMPLETELY STRAIGHT (zero bend, locked extension, RIGID — like vertical stiff sticks). Both legs parallel and held side-by-side. Ankles in plantarflexion (toes pointed DOWN at the floor like a ballerina). The whole body is rigid and vertical, like a pogo stick rod. Arms held at sides with slight elbow bend for balance. Torso completely upright (vertical). Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. NEGATIVE: this is NOT running, NOT jogging, NOT a normal jump with bent knees — knees DO NOT BEND AT ALL, only the ankles work. The body looks like a stiff stick jumping straight up.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: calf muscles (gastrocnemius and soleus) for elastic recoil and ankle stiffness. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: quadriceps and core muscles for body rigidity. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腓肠肌 / 比目鱼肌" pointing to the calf muscles for elastic recoil; "股四头肌" pointing to the quadriceps; "核心肌群" pointing to the core muscles. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
